items.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
items.summary: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. items.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
items.summary: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

items.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
items.summary: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

items.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
items.summary: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI c

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
items.summary: Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade secrets by OpenAI employees who were formerly at Apple." In addition to OpenAI, the lawsuit also names IO Prod

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
items.summary: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

items.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
items.summary: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. items.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
items.summary: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

items.title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
items.summary: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.

items.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
items.summary: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. items.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
items.summary: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let y

items.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
items.summary: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute n

items.title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
items.summary: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have

items.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
items.summary: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.summary: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

items.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
items.summary: Microsoft may once again be struggling to keep up with its own climate goals, according to its 2026 sustainability report. As reported by GeekWire, the report states that Microsoft's carbon emissions increased 25 percent in 2025, totalling 34 million metric tons "without select interventions." Micro

items.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 3): Attention is all you profile
Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
items.summary: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

items.title: Fidji Simo steps down from leading OpenAI’s AGI work due to illness
items.summary: OpenAI's Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as the company's AGI chief and is transitioning to being a "part-time advisor," she said on X. The news follows Simo's original announcement in April that she would take a few weeks of medical leave due to a neuroimmune condition, shortly after she

items.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

items.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
items.summary: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

items.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
items.summary: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

items.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
items.summary: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

items.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
items.summary: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

items.title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
items.summary: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."

items.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
items.summary: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is target

items.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the

items.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
items.summary: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

items.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
items.summary: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can

items.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

items.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
items.summary: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

items.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
items.summary: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

items.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
items.summary: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

items.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
items.summary: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

items.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
items.summary: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

items.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
items.summary: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

items.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
items.summary: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

items.title: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
items.summary: Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of security updates included in each security release."
